narrative.headline: What 2015 Chevrolet Equinox owners report most
narrative.summary: Visibility and wiper complaints dominate owner reports, with drivers describing glare from interior chrome trim reflecting sunlight and obscuring forward vision. Engine complaints are nearly as common, with owners reporting sudden, complete loss of power while driving — including steering, lights, and accessories — requiring a restart to recover. Electrical system and powertrain complaints round out the picture, involving unexpected automatic braking events and vehicle movement without driver input.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Owners report that sunlight reflecting off interior chrome trim onto the dashboard area obscures the driver's forward vision while the vehicle is in motion. · 1 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle suddenly losing all power while driving — including steering, lights, and radio — requiring a restart to regain function. · 4 fire · 2 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report incidents in which the vehicle moved without the driver present or in control, resulting in property damage or collision. · 4 crash

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the forward collision alert activating and the brakes applying suddenly and forcefully under clear road conditions with no obstacle present. · 1 crash
